response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with an impressive web and full-stack background at companies like Stripe and Shopify, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements of this role. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database experience is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets. His low-latency DLang work is technically adjacent but does not substitute for the specific C++ and financial-domain depth this consulting engagement demands.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic)
Strong general engineering craftsmanship — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — and senior tenure at top-tier companies (Stripe, Shopify)
Some professional C/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI) plus fluent English and demonstrated communication skills through workshops and talks
concerns: Only about seven months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development
No SQL Server or Oracle experience — database background is limited to MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; recent career trajectory is web/full-stack TypeScript, moving away from systems programming

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong low-latency systems and engineering fundamentals
